channel.title: SOC on bubka hacks stuff
channel.description: Recent content in SOC on bubka hacks stuff

item.title: Wazuh: SQL Injection Detection
item.description: SOC L1 investigation of a SQL Injection attack detected by Wazuh SIEM and Suricata IDS against DVWA

item.title: Wazuh: SQL Injection Detection
item.description: Detected a SQL Injection attack from Kali Linux against DVWA using sqlmap, observed 85 alerts across 6 rule IDs, and configured automated IP blocking via active response.

item.title: HTB-Telly
item.description: An attacker exploited CVE-2026-24061, an authentication bypass in GNU inetutils telnetd, to obtain an unauthenticated root shell, established persistence via linper.sh across multiple cron and systemd locations, and exfiltrated a credit card database before deleting it from the victim server.

item.title: Suricata + Wazuh: Malware Traffic
item.description: Replayed a malicious PCAP file containing Hancitor dropper traffic that deployed Cobalt Strike, Dridex, and Ficker Stealer. Analyzed 423 Suricata alerts in Wazuh, reconstructed the full infection chain, and mapped findings to MITRE ATT&CK.

item.title: HTB-GhostTrace
item.description: Analyzed Windows Event Logs revealing a complete AD compromise chain: phishing email with macro-enabled document → credential dumping with Mimikatz → lateral movement via PsExec → DCSync attack → domain admin compromise → persistence via scheduled task, service, and registry run key.

item.title: HTB-Packet_Puzzle
item.description: Analyzed network traffic showing exploitation of CVE-2024-4577 (PHP-CGI argument injection) against a Windows server running PHP 8.1.25. Attacker achieved RCE, established reverse shell on port 4545, then escalated privileges using GodPotato to spawn a SYSTEM-level shell on port 5555.
